description: §42.3.5 / §42.3.6 absence-safe render, PRESENT path. A plain-optional cell initialized present (`{ name: "Ada" }`) is read two absence-safe ways: `${@user?.name}` (optional-chain, §42.3.6) renders the value; the `if=@user`-narrowed `${@user.name}` (§42.4 narrowing) renders inside the mounted guard. Both compile clean (no E-TYPE-046) and render "Ada". Twin of optional-member-access-absent.
rationale: §42.3.6: `recv?.field` lowers to JS `?.` and yields the field value when the receiver is present. §42.4: `if=@user` narrows `@user` to the struct inside the guarded subtree, so the bare `${@user.name}` is safe and mounts. Neither form fires E-TYPE-046.
